## Highlights

Feature release on top of [0.5.3](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/tag/0.5.3).
Four quality-of-life improvements aimed at the lab's primary use case — iterating
on configuration-management code (Salt, Ansible) against throwaway VMs — plus a
clearer preflight error and a more informative `lvlab init` table.

- **In-guest `/etc/hosts` management is opt-out (#120).** Set
    `cloud_init.manage_etc_hosts: false` (in `config_defaults.cloud_init` or
    per-machine) and lvlab will leave the guest's hosts file alone — both
    halves at once: it renders `manage_etc_hosts: false` in the cloud-init
    `user-data` AND skips the two runcmd heredocs that rewrite `/etc/hosts` +
    the distro `hosts.{debian,redhat}.tmpl`. The natural setting when your
    CM tool owns `/etc/hosts` and you don't want lvlab fighting it. Default
    is `true` (today's behaviour) — fully backwards-compatible. See the
    walkthrough's "Disabling in-guest `/etc/hosts` management" section.

- **`lvlab ssh-config` is ephemeral-lab friendly by default (#127).** Each
    `Host` block now carries `StrictHostKeyChecking no` +
    `UserKnownHostsFile /dev/null` + `CheckHostIP no` + `LogLevel ERROR`, so
    a recycled DHCP-pool IP picking up a new host key on each lab cycle no
    longer trips `ssh`'s "REMOTE HOST IDENTIFICATION HAS CHANGED" or
    poisons `~/.ssh/known_hosts`. Pass `--strict-host-keys` to get the
    legacy snippet (`Host` / `HostName` / `User` / `IdentityFile` only) if
    you'd rather keep strict checking.

- **`lvlab init` shows a best-guess version column (#124).** A `version`
    column now sits between `image` and `phase`, parsing the upstream
    naming convention so you can tell which dated build (Debian
    `20260518-2482`), which release-build (Fedora `44-1.7`), which codename
    (Ubuntu `jammy` / `noble`), which `latest` tree (AlmaLinux
    `10 (latest)`, Debian `trixie/latest`) you actually cached. Pure
    string heuristics — no extra downloads, never crashes, falls back to
    `?` for unrecognised formats.

- **Smoke preflight: static-IP / DHCP overlap message is now scannable (#128).**
    When the manifest's static IPs collide with the libvirt network's DHCP
    range, the failure message is structured (network / subnet / DHCP range
    / conflicts / free band) and explicitly names BOTH remedies (narrow the
    DHCP range OR move the static IPs into the free band) — previously it
    only mentioned one. Free band(s) are computed and shown; the guard
    still blocks by default.

## Breaking changes

None. Every change is either default-preserving or additive:

- `#120` defaults `manage_etc_hosts` to `true` — pre-0.5.4 behaviour.
- `#127` is default-on, but the only behavioural change is in the
    snippet emitted by `lvlab ssh-config`; if you already appended an
    old snippet to your `~/.ssh/config`, it keeps working — re-run
    `lvlab ssh-config` to pick up the new opts (or pass
    `--strict-host-keys` to keep the old shape).
- `#124` adds a column; raw outputs unchanged.
- `#128-A` only restructures the failure message; the pass/fail decision
    is identical.

## Install / upgrade

```bash
uv tool install --upgrade \
  https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/download/0.5.4/tkc_lvlab-0.5.4-py3-none-any.whl
```

## Supported hosts

Unchanged from 0.5.3 — Debian 12 (3.11), Debian 13 (3.13), AlmaLinux 10
(3.12), Fedora 44 (3.14). See
[`docs-extra/host-validation.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.4/docs-extra/host-validation.md)
for the matrix.

**Validation note:** the autonomous overnight session covering this
release landed on unit-test coverage only — the `sudo salt` smoke loop
the maintainer normally runs on the 4-host matrix was out-of-scope for
the agent, so the four changes have **not yet been run end-to-end on
real libvirt**. Code-only diffs are mechanical; the two guest-behaviour
changes (`#120` `manage_etc_hosts`, `#128` smoke preflight message) are
called out in the per-issue close comments for the maintainer's morning
hardware-validate pass.
